ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: NEWTON LAM, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JOHN SANGER AND CATHERINE SANGER'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL FAILED TO MEET HIS OBLIGATIONS UNDER CCP 128.7(B)(3) AND MAINTAINED THIS ACTION AFTER BEING INFORMED THAT ALLEGATIONS OF THE DISCOVERY OF EASEMENT RECORDATION HAVE NO EVIDENTIARY SUPPORT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL COMPENSATE DEFENDANT FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED IN FILING ANSWER AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: NEWTON LAM, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michelle Scott (650-814-8479) Law Offices of Edward Singer for Defendants/Moving party JOHN SANGER and CATHERINE SANGER.
No other appearances.

Not Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs JOHN SANGER and CATHERINE SANGER's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel
is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's counsel failed to meet his obligations under CCP 128.7(b)(3) and maintained this action after being informed that allegations of the discovery of easement recordation have no evidentiary support. Plaintiff's counsel shall compensate Defendant for reasonable attorney's fees incurred in filing answer and motion for summary judgment. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Newton Lam, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

INTERVENOR WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT, LLC's Motion Of Waterview Development Llc To Intervene is DENIED in part and CONTINUED in part. Motion for mandatory intervention is DENIED. Moving party provides no authority or evidence that its interests are not adequately represented by the existing parties. Motion for discretionary leave to intervene is continued to July 7, 2017. Plaintiff and the moving party to file briefs (not to exceed 3 pages) to address the outcome of trial in action 536038 no later than July 3, 2017 by 4:00 p.m. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
